Aphelios.trading_pattern: Caitlyn dominates early with range and headshot pressure. Aphelios scales but Caitlyn's poke is relentless early.
Aphelios.ability_to_dodge: Caitlyn Headshot (auto attacks) — stay behind minions, never face-check bushes.

power_spikes.you: Level 2-3 with Headshot stacks, RFC for extended range
power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 with full weapons, Kraken Slayer completed

Aphelios.early_game: Dominate with superior range. Set traps in river and jungle entrance to control space.
Aphelios.mid_game: Aphelios scales but you maintain lane pressure. Group and leverage numbers advantage.
Aphelios.late_game: Aphelios outscales your damage. Position safely and rely on utility traps.
Aphelios.win_condition: Build an early lead through range dominance, translate to objectives before Aphelios scales.
Aphelios.scaling: Aphelios outscales — you dominate early, he dominates late
Aphelios.positioning: Maintain maximum range advantage. Use traps to control river and jungle entrances.

Ashe.trading_pattern: Caitlyn out-ranges and wins short trades. Ashe's slows allow safe trades but Caitlyn pressure is consistent.
Ashe.ability_to_dodge: Ashe R (Enchanted Crystal Arrow) — dodge by moving unpredictably, never stand still in river.

power_spikes.you: Level 2-3 with Headshot, RFC for extended range
power_spikes.enemy: Level 6 with ultimate slow, completed RFC

Ashe.early_game: Use range advantage to poke and pressure. Force her to farm at tower.
Ashe.mid_game: Maintain pressure but watch for ultimate ganks. Position away from river danger.
Ashe.late_game: Both scale well but Ashe's utility exceeds your single-target damage. Position to avoid her ult.
Ashe.win_condition: Build early lead through poke, then play around her ultimate threat.
Ashe.scaling: Ashe scales better late — both have utility but hers is more impactful
Ashe.positioning: Keep maximum range. Dodge her ultimate by moving unpredictably in river.

Caitlyn.trading_pattern: Mirror matchup where trap placement and headshot positioning matter most. Equal stats, skill-based.
Caitlyn.ability_to_dodge: Caitlyn E (90 Caliber Net) — stay mobile and never get stuck near walls for her to trap.

power_spikes.you: Level 2-3 with Headshot stacks, RFC for extended range
power_spikes.enemy: Same as you — scaling is identical

Caitlyn.early_game: Trade carefully and place traps strategically. Better trap placement wins fights.
Caitlyn.mid_game: Both maintain similar pressure. Team fighting and support matchup determine advantage.
Caitlyn.late_game: Whoever has better trap utilization and positioning wins late fights.
Caitlyn.win_condition: Outplay opponent's trap placement, position better in team fights.
Caitlyn.scaling: Perfectly even — identical in every way
Caitlyn.positioning: Use traps strategically to control crucial areas. Mirror opponent's range positioning.

Draven.trading_pattern: Draven out-trades with catchaxe stacks but Caitlyn's range and traps keep distance safe.
Draven.ability_to_dodge: Draven E (Cataclysm) — use E to reposition away from stun, never get caught against walls.

power_spikes.you: Level 2-3 with Headshot, RFC for extended range
power_spikes.enemy: Level 1-3 with stacked axes, early completed Duskblade

Draven.early_game: Play safe around his axes. Use range to poke from distance. Trap jungle to prevent ganks.
Draven.mid_game: Draven can snowball hard but Caitlyn's utility keeps you safe. Group and use numbers.
Draven.late_game: Caitlyn scales better. He falls off without lead. Position to never get caught.
Draven.win_condition: Survive early axes and snowball, scale into mid-game utility advantage.
Draven.scaling: Caitlyn outscales — Draven is early game champion
Draven.positioning: Keep distance from Draven at all times. Use E to escape his E stun.

Ezreal.trading_pattern: Caitlyn out-ranges and out-trades Ezreal heavily. His poke is weak compared to your damage.
